  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "To “make this stronger,” which focus do you prefer? A) Rhetorical polish only (tighter wording/flow; no structural changes or new citations); B) Argument and contribution strengthening (reframe thesis, make evidence–claim links explicit, possibly minor reordering; minimal citation work); or C) Scholarship positioning (add/verify citations and explicit novelty delta vs named prior work; minimal wording changes). Reply with A, B, or C (or a combination).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening may require sharpening the core contribution and reframing the thesis around the reliability frontier and market eligibility.",
    "R1.2": "Tightening the argument chain from the frontier definition to feasibility and market-qualification conclusions is often key to a stronger section.",
    "R1.3": "Because the passage asserts specific feasibility thresholds and comparative outcomes, ensuring claim–evidence alignment is central.",
    "R1.6": "A stronger presentation typically depends on more forceful, concise prose and improved sentence/paragraph flow.",
    "RX.1": "Ambiguity in the request warrants explicit acknowledgment of interpretive uncertainty.",
    "RX.2": "The text includes broad claims (e.g., no practical advantage), so we must avoid overstating beyond what evidence supports.",
    "RX.3": "We should adhere to the scope of strengthening this passage without introducing new analyses or results.",
    "RX.4": "Overstated or misaligned claims about market eligibility or strategy performance could cause downstream misuse.",
    "RX.5": "Any strengthening should remain traceable to the artifact’s stated framework, figures, and reported thresholds."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: To “make this stronger,” which focus do you prefer? A) Rhetorical polish only (tighter wording/flow; no structural changes or new citations); B) Argument and contribution strengthening (reframe thesis, make evidence–claim links explicit, possibly minor reordering; minimal citation work); or C) Scholarship positioning (add/verify citations and explicit novelty delta vs named prior work; minimal wording changes). Reply with A, B, or C (or a combination).

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening may require sharpening the core contribution and reframing the thesis around the reliability frontier and market eligibility.
rationale.R1.2: Tightening the argument chain from the frontier definition to feasibility and market-qualification conclusions is often key to a stronger section.
rationale.R1.3: Because the passage asserts specific feasibility thresholds and comparative outcomes, ensuring claim–evidence alignment is central.
rationale.R1.6: A stronger presentation typically depends on more forceful, concise prose and improved sentence/paragraph flow.
rationale.RX.1: Ambiguity in the request warrants explicit acknowledgment of interpretive uncertainty.
rationale.RX.2: The text includes broad claims (e.g., no practical advantage), so we must avoid overstating beyond what evidence supports.
rationale.RX.3: We should adhere to the scope of strengthening this passage without introducing new analyses or results.
rationale.RX.4: Overstated or misaligned claims about market eligibility or strategy performance could cause downstream misuse.
rationale.RX.5: Any strengthening should remain traceable to the artifact’s stated framework, figures, and reported thresholds.
